design-audit.message: Design audit not yet performed — run /rq:design-audit for UI audit

qa.message: QA testing not yet performed — run /rq:qa for browser testing

findings_details.title: Monotonically-increasing-deltas test uses inequality assertions that cannot catch delta-swap regressions
findings_details.body: Lines 39-40 assert toBeGreaterThan(0) and toBeGreaterThanOrEqual — these pass even if totalDeltaMs and firstMessageDeltaMs were swapped, or if totalDeltaMs accidentally equalled firstMessageDeltaMs due to a clock-sampling bug. The advancing fake clock produces deterministic values (firstMessageDeltaMs=10, totalDeltaMs=40 with the +10 increment pattern) so exact equality assertions are feasible and much stronger.

findings_details.title: startedAtMs field is never asserted in any test
findings_details.body: All 8 tests check delta fields (firstMessageDeltaMs, totalDeltaMs, etc.) and counts, but none asserts startedAtMs. This field is the epoch timestamp anchoring all deltas and is used for log ordering in TimingsLogLine. A bug that set startedAtMs incorrectly (e.g. to 0 or to the end time) would go undetected. Test 1 uses a fixed clock of () => 1000 so expect(calls[0].startedAtMs).toBe(1000) is a free one-liner assertion.

findings_details.title: No test for early consumer abandonment (break mid-stream)
findings_details.body: The try/finally in timedStream guarantees a timing record is pushed even when the consumer breaks out of the for-await loop early (the generator's return() method triggers finally). This is a documented invariant — records survive even partial iteration — but no test exercises it. A future refactor that accidentally moved the records.push() outside the finally block would silently break this guarantee. A test that iterates with a manual for-await + break after the first message, then checks getRecordedTimings().length === 1, would pin the invariant.

findings_details.title: First appendTimingsLog test does not assert on the ts field
findings_details.body: The test at line 52-72 asserts parsed[runId], parsed[agentId], parsed[status], and parsed[timings], but never checks parsed[ts]. ts is a required field of TimingsLogLine (not optional) and is the primary timestamp used for log ordering. A typo in the serialisation key (e.g. timestamp instead of ts) would go undetected. Fix: add expect(parsed[ts]).toBe(1_700_000_000) after line 72.
